column1: QUES
column2: A
column3: B
column4: C
column5: D

column1: Which of the listed requirements for cargo ships regarding personal life-saving appliances does not correspond to present regulations?
column2: Ships fitted with free-fall life-boats may carry thermal protective aids instead of immersion suits.
column3: Self-igniting lights for lifebuoys required on tankers shall be of an electric battery type. (Correct Answer - chemical lights are prohibited on tankers)
column4: Ships with a length of 100 metres or less shall carry minimum 8 lifebuoys.
column5: Cargo ships of less than 85 metres in length shall, as a general rule, carry immersion suits for every person onboard.

column1: Which item among those below is included in the normal equipment of every lifeboat?
column2: one immersion suit for each person
column3: anti-seasickness medicine and one seasickness bag for each person (Correct Answer)
column4: a VHF transceiver
column5: sea-charts and navigating equipment

column1: An inflatable lifejacket will inflate:
column2: either automatically on immersion or by a single manual motion or by mouth
column3: automatically on immersion
column4: either automatically on immersion or by a single manual motion (Correct Answer - mouth inflation is a back-up, but the "inflation mechanism" refers to the CO2 system)
column5: either automatically on immersion or by mouth

column1: How often shall each member of the crew participate in an "abandon ship"-drill?
column2: Once a year
column3: Once every 6 months
column4: Once every week
column5: Once every month (Correct Answer)

column1: Which one of the listed requirements regarding rocket parachute flares do not correspond to present regulations? The rocket parachute flares shall:
column2: be contained in a water-resistant casing
column3: have integral means of ignition
column4: burn with a bright orange colour (Correct Answer - They must burn with a bright red colour; orange is for smoke signals)
column5: have brief instructions clearly illustrating the use of the flare

column1: Which one of the listed requirements regarding launching appliances for survival crafts and rescue boats do not correspond to present regulations?
column2: All launching appliances shall be capable of recovering survival crafts and rescue boats with their crew (Correct Answer - Only rescue boat appliances are strictly required to recover the craft with crew; survival craft/lifeboat appliances are primarily for launching)
column3: The launching mechanism shall be arranged for actuating from a position within the survival craft or rescue boat in addition to a position on the ship's deck
column4: Launching appliances shall be constructed for minimum amount of routine maintenance
column5: Parts requiring regular maintenance by ships crew shall be readily accessible

column1: Where do you find instructions for tasks and duties in case of an emergency?
column2: In instruction notices posted in the crew's cabins.
column3: In instruction notices posted in messrooms, recreation rooms etc.
column4: On posters at the lifeboat station.
column5: In the muster list. (Correct Answer)

column1: The muster list shall specify details of general alarm signal and also actions to be taken by crew and passengers when the alarm is sounded. Which of the following actions do not necessarily have to be included in the muster list? The muster list shall:
column2: specify which officers are assigned to ensure that life-saving and fire-fighting appliances are maintained in good condition
column3: specify substitutes for key persons who may become disabled
column4: be prepared and approved by the Administration before the ship proceeds to sea (Correct Answer - It is prepared by the Master/Company; it doesn't require individual "approval" by the Administration for every voyage)
column5: specify how the order to abandon ship will be given

column1: What kind of safety equipment must be worn during mooring operations?
column2: Dress according to the climate.
column3: All of the mentioned equipment. (Correct Answer)
column4: Safety helmet, safety shoes.
column5: Boiler suit, working gloves.

column1: Question: Give the meaning of the following symbol
column2: Foam installation
column3: Portable foam installation
column4: Powder installation
column5: Sprinkler installation

column1: What is the correct procedure to extinguish a soot fire in the exhaust boiler?
column2: Increase speed or continue full speed
column3: Reduce speed and use soot blowers if some are fitted
column4: Stop the engine and let the fire burn out by itself
column5: Stop the engine and use the CO2 fixed fire-extinguishing system

column1: Instructions for "Preparation for welding or hot work in cargo tanks" is laid down by the Norwegian Maritime Directorate. During ongoing inspection on work in cargo tanks, the gas concentration shall be checked:
column2: Every fourth hour.
column3: Every hour.
column4: Every 30 minutes.
column5: Every second hour.

column1: Which one of the given requirements listed below, regarding survival craft muster and embarkation arrangements, listed below, corresponds to the present SOLAS regulations?
column2: muster and embarkation stations shall be readily accessible from accommodation and work areas
column3: searchlights to be provided at the launching station
column4: davit-launched survival craft muster and embarkation stations shall not be arranged to enable stretchers to be placed in survival craft
column5: muster and embarkation stations are to be arranged separately to improve working conditions

column1: The charge to the cost for a message sent by the Inmarsat-C installation must be calculated. The size of the message is 2,56kbits. The charges message sent is 0,17 SDR per unit. The total charge for the message is:
column2: 11 times 0,17 SDR
column3: 2,56 times 0,17 SDR
column4: Don't know
column5: 3 times 0,17 SDR

column1: As a fire fighter, what should you always consider when fighting a fire in a confined space?
column2: The confined space always has six sides
column3: The confined space can be considered to have one side only as heat always rises
column4: The confined space has only five sides
column5: The confined space has only four sides

column1: On board they want to have a dial-phone call via Inmarsat with the Apollogracht. In the guides the following ID's are found for the Apollogracht: 344320000, 424432010, 424432020, 1300210, 36715. What ID should be chosen:
column3: Don't know

column1: Which fire-fighting equipment is most efficient and with least side effects in case of a large fire in the engine room?
column2: Powder extinguishing system
column3: Central gas extinguishing system and/or waterfog.
column4: Central foam extinguishing system
column5: Sprinkler system

column1: The fire point is
column2: The minimum temperature at which a combustible gives off vapours that may burn when they are in contact with oxygen in air, when there is a presence of activation energy; but with no capacity of maintaining combustion.
column3: A process of chemical decomposition by oxidation-reduction at unusually high temperature, in areas with slight ventilation.
column4: The minimum temperature at which a combustible gives off vapours that may burn in contact with oxygen in air, and at which combustion persists once the source of ignition has been removed
column5: The maximum pressure that will be reached when a given combustible burns into an enclosed space

column1: A particular vessel is equipped with the possibility to insert CO2 in the scavenging air belt in case of uncontrolled fire. What are the action to be taken in case of fire in the scavenging air belt?
column2: Reduce the RPM waiting for the fire to burn out, during continuous monitoring of condition.
column3: Continue running the engine on regular speed waiting for the fire to burn out, during continuous monitoring of condition.
column4: Continue running the engine on regular speed inserting CO2 awaiting the fire to burn out.
column5: Reduce the RPM to reduce the scavenge air temperature. When the temperature is reduced and in case the fire is not extinguished by itself, insert carefully CO2.

column1: You are Sen.Off.Deck on a vessel and you have collided with another vessel which is on fire. What would you do?
column2: Commence arguing with the other Sen.Off.Deck about the reason for the collision
column3: Leave the other vessel because the other vessel was to blame for the collision
column4: Check own vessel for any loss of life, danger of explosion and offer your assistance to the other vessel
column5: Stay close to the other vessel and commence fire fighting procedures

column1: When the Sen. Deck Off. of both vessels involved have decided which will be the last lines to let go of whilst unmooring, these lines should be made fast as follows on the ship accepting the lines.
column2: By keeping the rope on the warping drum of a deck winch, fig. 3
column3: By turning up the rope on the bitts, fig. 1
column4: By putting the eye on the bitts, fig. 2
column5: By shackling the eye to a strongpoint, fig. 4

column1: You are sailing in shallow water, with 15% Under Keel Clearance. How will the stopping distance be, compared to deep water?
column2: About 1 ship-length longer
column3: The same as in all depths
column4: Shorter
column5: Longer

column1: Your engine is going astern and you pick up sternway. The rudder is midships, and you are operating on a single, right-handed fixed screw. How will your ship react?
column2: She will most likely change heading to port
column3: The stern will be lifted to starboard
column4: She will most likely go straight astern
column5: She will most likely change heading to starboard (Note: Due to "transverse thrust" or "prop walk," the stern typically swings to port, causing the bow/heading to swing to starboard).

column1: During a double banking operation the off-take or smaller vessel generally provides the moorings. If wires are used it is recommended that ropetails are fitted onto the ends of the wires. This is to:
column2: Permit quick cutting in an emergency
column3: Introduce electrical discontinuity between ships
column4: All the listed alternatives
column5: Introduce some elasticity into the lines for shock load absorption

column1: Ship to ship communication during SAR should be executed by?
column2: MF
column3: VHF
column4: Satellite
column5: VHF and MF

column1: What is most important when transferring route plans to other nav. systems?
column2: Both systems use same chart datum
column3: Both systems are approved by the classification society
column4: Both systems work according to specification
column5: Both systems are made by the same manufacturer

column1: Who is responsible for the voyage plan?
column2: The person who has done the planning
column3: The navigation officer
column4: The Master
column5: The owner

column1: Does most piracy attacks take place in territorial or international waters?
column2: Most attacks take place in territorial waters
column3: Most attacks take place in international waters
column4: Number of attacks are approximately 50/50% in international and territorial waters
column5: The most serious attacks take place in international waters

column1: How many, minimum lines of positions are needed to decide a position using the decca system?
column2: 8 lines of position.
column3: 2 lines of position.
column4: 1 line of position.
column5: 6 lines of position.

column1: Which of the four calculations below is the correct set up, if you want to find observed latitude when the sun is on the meridian?
column2: Calculation 3 is correct.
column3: Calculation 2 is correct.
column4: Calculation 1 is correct.
column5: Calculation 4 is correct.

column1: In celestial navigation the angle measured westward from the first point of aries to the hour circle of the body along the celestial equator is known as:
column2: Azimuth angle
column3: Local sidereal time
column4: Greenwich hour angle
column5: Sidereal hour angle

column1: Your vessel is a laden tanker and has collided with another tanker causing them to interlock. What action/judgement should be taken first of all?
column2: Separate the two vessels immediately
column3: Reduce cargo content by dropping into empty/slack tank
column4: Take separation of the two vessels into immediate consideration, but consider that a separation may create a larger oil spill
column5: Shut down non-essential air intakes

column1: You are the Duty officer on vessel "A" in US Fairway waters. According to your radar observation you will pass tug "B" at which time you will be close to vessel "D". How will you make this situation safe?
column2: By steering outside the fairway.
column3: By passing stern of "B" and "D"
column4: Wait and see how the situation develops
column5: By reducing your speed until vessel "D" is cleared

column1: Your vessel "A" is bound for the Pilot station. Vessel "B" will be picking up the pilot before you. According to your observations you are getting too close to vessel "B" and Pilot boat "C". What would you do to avoid a collision?
column2: Observe the situation during a period of time and see how its developing, replace "will" with "would"
column3: Call vessel "B" and inform them to turn around to starboard
column4: Try to make a port turn
column5: Full astern and drop the anchor

column1: You are on duty on vessel "A", to avoid collision in the described situation, how will you act?
column2: By giving the vessel a port turn
column3: Wait and see. Maybe the situation will be cleared by itself
column4: By giving the vessel a starboard turn
column5: By stopping the engine

column1: What is a "Rescue Sub-Centre" (RSC)?
column2: A center established to assist the main national rescue centre
column3: A unit subordinate to a rescue co-ordinating centre
column4: A centre for underwater search and rescue operation
column5: A national search and rescue centre subordinate to an international search and rescue co-ordinating centre

column1: You are Sen.Off.Deck onboard a merchant vessel. During the night you have been in a collision with a sail boat and there are no signs of life in the water. What would you do?
column2: Alarm the rescue centre and commence searching for any survivors
column3: Leave the position as fast as possible
column4: Call the company, explain the situation and ask for orders
column5: Do nothing and wait for assistance

column1: What is the search pattern system called where 1 ship and 1 plane are involved?
column2: Patterton
column3: SOLAS
column4: Flight Cross
column5: Coordinated Crab (Note: In standard IAMSAR, this is often called a Coordinated Search, but "Coordinated Crab" is the specific answer for this test software's terminology.)

column1: When using a buoy as an aid to navigation, which of the following should always be considered?
column2: That if the light is showing its correct characteristic it is in its right position
column3: The buoy may not be in its charted position
column4: The buoy is nearly always well anchored and can be considered to be in its charted position providing there has not been any recent storms in the area
column5: If no radio navigation warnings have been issued concerning the buoy it can be assumed to be in its correct position

column1: Most cases of oil pollution are caused by human errors, often jointly by seafarers and shore based clean-up teams. What has proved to be the essential mistake executed by the shore people regarding environment damages?
column2: Dispersement of chemicals?
column3: Usage of sawdust?
column4: Usage of oilbooms?
column5: Usage of absorbent pads?

column1: What is the result of a "high metacentric height"?
column2: The vessel's tweendeck heights is too high?
column3: The vessel will roll violently?
column4: The vessel will roll slowly or be unstable?
column5: The vessel will have a great bending moment?

column1: Which of the following requirements to ships stability for normal operation corresponds to present regulations? (NSCL 4/12.2)
column2: Unless othereays stated in the approved stability calculation, the total weight of the deck cargo shall not exeed 50 metric tons.
column3: The ship is loaded in such a manner that adequate stability is achieved in all loading condition.
column4: I waters with the danger of icing, loading of deck cargo must be approved by competent authority.
column5: Centre of gravity shall be calculated with an accurancy better than 5 percent.

column1: Small oil spills on deck can be kept from going overboard by doing what?
column2: Driving wooden plugs into the vents
column3: Plugging the sounding pipes
column4: Plugging the scuppers
column5: Closing the lids on the vents

column1: What are bending moments?
column2: Forces affecting between loaded and empty parts of the vessel.
column3: Forces affecting the aft part of the vessel.
column4: Forces which bends the vessel up and down in the fore-aft direction (hogg/sagg).
column5: Forces affecting the fore part of the vessel.

column1: It is important that a compress is done correctly. One of the alternative signs, after completed compress bandage, shows that the bandage may be to tight. Which one?
column2: Swelling above the bandage.
column3: Blue colour of the skin above the bandage.
column4: Heartbeat increase and a dizzy feeling.
column5: Swelling below the bandage and heavy pain.

column1: For cargo calculations there are two corrections to be made. Two tables are used for this purpose.
column2: List and trim correction tables.
column3: Shear and bending tables.
column4: Cargo calibration tables.
column5: Pressure and vacuum tables.

column1: Your vessel has been involved in a collision, which has resulted in a heavy oil leakage. Who should be called to handle pollution claims and damages?
column2: The Classification Society's representative.
column3: Flag state representative.
column4: The P & I Club's nearest representative.
column5: The Leading Hull Underwriter's nearest Average Agent.

column1: If pollution takes place, what would you do immediately?
column2: Inform the vessel's agent.
column3: Report to relevant authorities.
column4: Inform the manager.
column5: Start clean-up operations

column1: In what kind of atmosphere should tank washing be carried out onboard vessels with inert-gas system?
column2: After the bottom has been covered with salt water.
column3: Steamed to a humidity of at least 75%
column4: After an electric fan have been blowing fresh air into the tank for a minimum time of one hour.
column5: Inerted to less than 8% oxygen(IMO).

column1: You are in charge of discharging and crude oil washing a crude oil carrier. What is the maximum allowed oxygen level measured one meter below deck in a tank during crude oil washing? (IMO Reg)
Which international convention deals with pollution prevention?
column2: MARPOL 73/78.
column3: SOLAS.
column4: ISGOTT.
column5: STCW 78.

column1: To supervise the pressure conditions in the cargo tanks, a special device is fitted for this purpose. What is it called?
column2: Negative/positive pressure valve.
column3: Supervising valve.
column4: Pressure/vacuum valve.
column5: Tank atmosphere control valve.

column1: During air venting/gas freeing of tanks the blowers use air from?
column2: The ventilation line
column3: The blower air inlet
column4: The scrubber
column5: The engine room

column1: On a flue gas plant, the flue gas enters a washing tower where the gas is washed to remove soot, ashes and to separate undesired gases. What is the name of this «washing tower»?
column2: The washing tower.
column3: Cleaning tower.
column4: Inert gas washer.
column5: Scrubber.

column1: In an inert gas system audible and visible alarms shall be provided to indicate faulty conditions in the installation. Which of the following conditions do not activate the alarms? (SOLAS II-2/62.19.1)
column2: High gas pressure
column3: High air pressure in water seal
column4: High water level in the flue gas scrubber
column5: High gas temperature

column1: What is the highest permitted back-pressure for the Cargo Pump when it is tested?
column2: 18.0 bar
column3: 1.25 times the maximum working pressure
column4: 5.0 bar
column5: 25.0 bar

column1: To discharge the last amount of cargo ashore, the stripping pump through the small diameter line is used. Where does this small diameter line come from in the pump room and where is the line ending at the manifold?
column2: From the cargo pump’s delivery side to the manifold’s presentation flange.
column3: From the stripping pump’s suction side to the manifold’s presentation flange.
column4: From the stripping pump’s delivery side to a particular shore line.
column5: From the stripping pump’s delivery side directly to the manifold’s presentation flange.

column1: The heat needed to transform a solid substance at a given temperature into a liquid substance with the same temperature is called, what?
column2: Condensation.
column3: Melting heat.
column4: Entalphy.
column5: Specific melting heat.

column1: What is the required pressure when testing parts of the oil transfer system (hoses, loading arms etc.) if the test is to satisfy U.S. Coast Guard regulations?
column2: Test pressure equal to the maximum allowable working pressure
column3: Test pressure equal to 1.25 times the maximum allowable working pressure
column4: Test pressure equal to 1.75 times the maximum allowable working pressure
column5: Test pressure equal to 1.5 times the maximum allowable working pressure

column1: Why does MARPOL require debottoming of tanks prior to COW (Crude Oil Washing)?
column2: To prevent corrosion in COW machines and COW piping
column3: To prevent corrosion of tanks
column4: In order not to mix crude and water
column5: To prevent electrostatic generation

column1: Fire protection for tankers of 20,000 dwt and upwards consist of different fixed systems and arrangements. Which of the following systems and arrangements do not correspond to the present regulations?
column2: A fixed inert gas system for all cargo tanks
column3: All tankers fitted with a fixed inert gas system shall be provided with a closed ullage system
column4: A fixed foam system for cargo deck area
column5: A fixed sprinkler system at the cargo manifold
